intro: Allium sativum elixir, also known as garlic elixir, is a liquid extract made from the bulb of the garlic plant. This elixir has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and antiviral properties, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular and digestive health. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Allium sativum elixir as a treatment for various health conditions.
side_effects: Allium sativum elixir can have side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. It may also cause anemia or low platelet count in some individuals. Consuming high doses of garlic, the main ingredient in this elixir, can lead to a stronger garlic breath and body odor.

recipe: To make allium sativum elixir for medicinal use, it is recommended to use 10-15 grams of fresh garlic (Allium sativum), chopped and soaked in 100ml of vodka or a high-proof spirit for at least 2 weeks, allowing the mixture to infuse and then strain the liquid before use. This elixir may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health due to the presence of compounds like allicin. The prepared elixir can be stored in a cool, dark place and consumed in small amounts as needed.
